card.hook: If you let untrusted YAML, pickle, jsonpickle, or affected protobuf-python inputs through, the system is already compromised before any trie exists. In Python 3.13 free-threaded deployments, the safer shape is parse once into pure data, compile once into an immutable trie, then share it for read-mostly access instead of relying on mutable built-in containers with internal locks.
card.why_it_matters: This gives you a concrete pattern to study for building Python data structures that preserve a security boundary while still supporting efficient prefix lookup and concurrent reads.
card.action: Write a short design note with two columns: approved ingestion paths (JSON/XML, YAML with Loader=yaml.SafeLoader, patched protobuf-python only) and rejected paths (pickle load/loads, PyYAML load on user-controlled input, jsonpickle, affected protobuf-python versions), then end it with one decision sentence on whether your index should target an immutable trie artifact.
